nodes.text: The court's application of the test laid down in Govind Saran Ganga Saran vs. CST, 1985, to the legislations in question is a novel extension of existing legal principles to determine their constitutionality.
nodes.id: Insight::/Users/akshat/Projects/Legal Agentic Engine/input_pdfs/The_State_Of_Kerala_vs_Asianet_Satellite_Communications_Ltd_on_22_May_2025.PDF::The court's application of the test laid down in G

nodes.text: The distinction of the Geeta Enterprises precedent on the basis of the specific interpretation of the word 'entertainment' in a different statutory context is an unusual treatment of a precedent, highlighting the importance of contextual analysis.
nodes.id: Insight::/Users/akshat/Projects/Legal Agentic Engine/input_pdfs/The_State_Of_Kerala_vs_Asianet_Satellite_Communications_Ltd_on_22_May_2025.PDF::The distinction of the Geeta Enterprises precedent

nodes.text: The court's observation that the levy of entertainment duty falls under Entry 62 - List II, raises questions about the logical limitations of this classification, particularly in relation to the taxation of intangible services.
nodes.id: Insight::/Users/akshat/Projects/Legal Agentic Engine/input_pdfs/The_State_Of_Kerala_vs_Asianet_Satellite_Communications_Ltd_on_22_May_2025.PDF::The court's observation that the levy of entertain

nodes.text: The court's failure to address the potential implications of its decision on the taxation of other intangible services, such as digital entertainment, is a significant omission that may have far-reaching consequences.
